title: CIA Chief: “I’m a Time Traveler!”
summary: Former CIA officer Ralph Moat Larson discusses his decades-long intelligence career, including briefing President George W. Bush on terrorism threats and nuclear weapons concerns, while also describing profound mystical experiences he claims include time travel to medieval Greece and prophetic dreams involving apocalyptic numbers.

acronyms.definition: United States government department that manages nuclear weapons and energy programs
Nuclear, biological, or chemical weapons capable of causing mass casualties
United States domestic intelligence and security service
Term used for unidentified flying objects

programs_mentioned: counterterrorism center weapons of mass destruction department

events.description: Invasion of Czechoslovakia referenced as context for Cold War tensions
Ralph Moat Larson went to Fort Knox as an armor officer before deployment to the Czech border
Ralph Moat Larson's wife had a dream sharing the same mystical numbers he had received
Anwar Sadat assassination on September 6th, which Larson claims to have dreamed about and witnessed
Ralph Moat Larson started his CIA career
Collapse of the Soviet Union and Larson's pilgrimage to Mount Athos, Greece where he claims to have time traveled
9/11 terrorist attacks on the Twin Towers
US invasion of Iraq; Larson briefed President Bush on terrorist Abu Musab Zarqawi's location three weeks before invasion
Larson began serving in the Middle East for his final CIA tour
Larson ended his CIA career after serving in the Middle East

key_claims.claim: Ralph Moat Larson claims to have physically time traveled to 14th century Mount Athos, Greece in 1991, experiencing it as indistinguishable from waking reality
The CIA and intelligence community knew they were going to be hit before 9/11 but didn't know where or when
Al-Qaeda was negotiating with Pakistani nuclear scientists to try to acquire a nuclear weapon for use in the United States
The Iraq WMD intelligence failure was based on bad sources who were fabricating information, not intentional deception by the administration
Most experienced people knew the Iraq invasion would turn out badly
Larson considered resigning when the US invaded Iraq because he was philosophically against the war
Larson advised President Bush to bomb the terrorist camp where Zarqawi was located
The Department of Energy has involvement in managing UFO secrecy and crash retrievals
Larson learned some of the nation's most important secrets at DOE, not CIA
Larson's wife independently dreamed the same mystical numbers (3, 6, 9, 12, 18, 27, 40, 49) that he had received in his dream
Larson found a grave of a German soldier named Frederick Wolf who died on November 5, 1944 - the same date as Larson's birthday
